surfaces.title: Tensor contracts and typed refusal vocabulary
surfaces.current_truth: typed tensor ids, dtypes, shapes, devices, quantization layouts, and refusal codes already exist as shared engine contracts
surfaces.extension_or_gap: no article-specific closure gap lives here; higher layers consume these contracts

surfaces.title: Lazy array facade and bounded public materialization surface
surfaces.current_truth: the repo already has a bounded graph-backed array surface with explicit evaluation, backend identities, cache posture, and debug capture hooks
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the owned article-equivalence stack still needs broader transformer-oriented tensor/view/math coverage than the current narrow public array surface provides

surfaces.title: Module state tree and basic reusable layers
surfaces.current_truth: module trees, parameter traversal, quantized wrappers, and reusable Linear/Embedding/LayerNorm/RmsNorm/Dropout layers already exist
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the owned article route still needs attention primitives and article-block composition above these layers

surfaces.title: Canonical reusable Transformer-boundary configs
surfaces.current_truth: the dedicated `psionic-transformer` crate now owns reusable decoder and AttnRes architecture primitives at the intended layering boundary
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the remaining gap is implementing the canonical article stack on top of this boundary, not redefining the boundary itself

surfaces.title: Model descriptors and weight-lineage metadata
surfaces.current_truth: shared model descriptors, weight bundle metadata, and artifact-governance wrappers already exist for reusable model families
surfaces.extension_or_gap: these surfaces can be reused directly once the canonical article model artifact exists

surfaces.title: AttnRes reference model family
surfaces.current_truth: the repo already has a bounded CPU-reference AttnRes family that is useful as a model-architecture pilot and training/control experiment surface
surfaces.extension_or_gap: it is not the canonical article route and remains a research-only forcing function rather than a closure blocker

surfaces.title: Existing executor-transformer scaffold
surfaces.current_truth: the repo already has executor-transformer configs, weight bundles, forward-pass scaffolding, decode state, KV points, and refusal posture
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the scaffold still needs paper-faithful architecture, owned vocabulary/channel binding, exactness closure, and lineage closure before it can become the canonical article route

surfaces.title: Fixture-backed article executor lane
surfaces.current_truth: the current fixture-backed article executor lane still carries the bounded Rust-only article closeout and compatibility truth
surfaces.extension_or_gap: canonical article-equivalence closure cannot stop at this reference-fixture lane; it must graduate to an owned Transformer-backed artifact

surfaces.title: Runtime proof identity and trace receipts
surfaces.current_truth: execution proof bundles, runtime identity, trace ABI contracts, trace artifacts, and replay-safe evidence surfaces already exist
surfaces.extension_or_gap: these runtime truth surfaces can be reused directly once the owned article route emits the right artifacts

surfaces.title: Article-closeout runtime and direct-proof receipts
surfaces.current_truth: the runtime already owns article ABI, article runtime closeout, and direct model-weight proof surfaces
surfaces.extension_or_gap: those receipts still need rebinding from the current bounded lanes into the canonical owned Transformer-backed article route

current_truth_boundary: psionic already owns real reusable substrate for tensor contracts, bounded array execution, module state, basic layers, transformer-boundary configs, model descriptors, weight lineage metadata, runtime proof identity, and trace receipts. The current gap is not substrate absence; it is the missing owned article-equivalence stack and the current presence of bounded research or fixture-backed lanes that are not yet sufficient for canonical article closure.
claim_boundary: this report inventories reusable substrate only. It freezes what can be reused, what needs extension, what stays research-only, and what is still not sufficient for article closure. It does not claim that the owned article-equivalence Transformer stack, the canonical article model artifact, or the final article-equivalence proof route already exist.
summary: Existing substrate inventory now records surface_count=10, blocker_surface_count=5, non_blocker_surface_count=5, inventory_contract_green=true, tied_requirement_satisfied=true, and article_equivalence_green=false.
